project.title: Proteome of renal tubuli and serum differentiate pre-existing type 2 diabetes and post-transplant diabetes in kidney transplant recipients
project.projectDescription: This study explores these differences by comparing the proteomes of proximal tubules and serum from normoglycemic (NG), pre-transplant T2DM, and PTDM patients one-year post-kidney transplantation.
project.sampleProcessingProtocol: Laser capture microdissection and proteomics sample preparation from FFPE tissue. Microdissected FFPE proximal tubuli were stored in 10µL of RIPA lysis buffer (cat. R0278; Sigma-Aldrich) with cOmplete Protease Inhibitor Cocktail (cat. 04693116001; Roche) at -80ºC until peptide extraction. The samples were then alkylated with 200mM iodoacetamide (30 min at room temperature, dark) and digested overnight at 37ºC with SP3 buffer (4M urea, 100mM HEPES, pH 8.5, in water) and a mass spectrometry grade Lys-C and Trypsin mix with a concentration of 0.1µg/µL (cat. no. V5071, Promega, Madison, WI, USA). Following digestion, samples were eluted with 2% DMSO, acidified with 1% methanoic acid and desalted in a 2mg Oasis C18 96-well plate (cat. no. 186001829, Waters, Milford, MA, USA). Samples were eluted with 80% acetonitrile, 0.1% methanoic acid and dried by a vacuum centrifuge before being resuspended in 0.5% formic acid acid and 2% acetonitrile. Next, the samples were tagged using tandem mass tags (TMT) dissolved in anhydrous acetonitrile (5µg/µL). The samples were then combined, dried by a vacuum centrifuge and desalted in a HLB Oasis 96-well plate (cat. no. 186000128, Waters, Milford, MA, USA). Subsequently, the combined samples were dried in a vacuum centrifuge.
project.dataProcessingProtocol: Data search using Proteome Discoverer. Bioinformatics using Perseus. DEPs, GO, and functional enrichment analysis.

references.referenceLine: Skandalou E, Rivedal M, Marti HP, Halden TAS, Jenssen T, Vikse BE, Åsberg A, Furriol J. Proteome of Renal Tubuli and Serum Differentiate Pre-Existing Type 2 Diabetes and Post-Transplant Diabetes in Kidney Transplant Recipients. Proteomics Clin Appl. 2025 19(3):e70000
